title: What I think about the Future 1/3

transcript.text: Hello, my name is Carl Pounds, and this is what I think about the future.
What, like, what's going to happen in the future?
We get together with our buddies every once in a while, and we talk about that kind of stuff.
But I think it's cool that we make videos for YouTube.
I'd love to hear what other people think.
And I think the number one rule, though, is that whatever future you envision, it should be beautiful and fit everything perfectly together.
Because up until now, I think it's been like that.
I think everything's been growing and evolving beautifully and perfectly.
I think we live in a much better place now than we did before.
I love technology and everything that's happening, so.
But there are definitely some, we are definitely on a path that has to change because we are still populating the Earth so fast.
I was looking at the statistics last night, and we're at 6 billion now, and I think they were projecting that by 2100, it'll be at 9 billion.
And I saw the graph from, I think, 1900 or something.
It's pretty steady all the way up until 2100, so.
It's not like we're growing exponentially.
But a lot of people think there's going to be a catastrophe from overpopulation and stripping of the Earth.
And India is projected to have, it's 1 billion, it hit 1 billion 10 years ago,
And it's projected to have 1.4 billion, I think, by 2050.
So, that's a big increase.
But, scientists say that the Earth should be able to sustain 20 billion people if we live, if we live properly, sustainably.
And there's lots of ways we can, I think, if we do it right, we can live sustainably with growing food, with aeroponics.
You can just make skyscraper buildings, pump water into them, not a whole lot, and just do aeroponics and have as much food as you want.
You can just make that practically free, have the cultivation of it fully automated, and the energy to get that water for, when you do it, when there's not a whole lot of water.
Well, I mean, if you're around the ocean, I guess, I think, well, that's a big, I think there's a million different ways to get energy.
It's just whatever way you happen to think of.
We're getting really strong batteries.
I think our batteries are still getting stronger at the same rate that, I don't, I mean, I'm not sure about this.
I should look it up, but I think batteries are getting stronger at the same rate, kind of exponentially, as a computer capacity is.
But anyways, batteries are getting really strong.
And there's tons of different ways to provide energy to those batteries.
Solar panels are getting stronger and stronger also.
